mission.title: the-update-framework-tuf-: Yearly maintainer list cleanup
mission.description: Yearly maintainer list cleanup. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check python-tuf installation
steps.description:
Verify your python-tuf installation:
```bash
pip show tuf
python3 -c "import tuf; print(tuf.__version__)"
```
Install if needed: `pip install tuf`. TUF (The Update Framework) is a Python library for secure software updates, not a Kubernetes workload.

steps.title: Review the issue context
steps.description:
Teodora stepping down as maintainer got me thinking that we should clean up the maintainers list periodically:
* Review both [docs/MAINTAINERS.txt](https://github.com/theupdateframework/python-tuf/blob/develop/docs/MAINTAINERS.txt) and the GitHub team permissions
* Every maintainer account increases attack surface, so periodic review is important

steps.title: Apply the fix for Yearly maintainer list cleanup
steps.description:
This is easy to forget as:
* There are multiple different critical services
* Some permissions are not visible to everyone

But review is important as every maintainer account increases attack surface.
So let's remind ourselves once a year.

This relates to #1793.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description:
Test that the fix is working as expected:
```bash
python3 -c "import tuf; print(tuf.__version__)"
pip show tuf
```
Confirm the feature described in "Yearly maintainer list cleanup" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Periodic maintainer list cleanup is important as every maintainer account increases attack surface. Multiple critical services have permissions that are not visible to everyone, so annual review ensures the maintainer list stays current.

prerequisites.description: Python 3.8+ with pip. TUF is a Python library, not a Kubernetes workload.
